Empires & Puzzles Chomper Review: The Hamster Brawler with a Healing Axe

Don't let the chubby cheeks fool you. Chomper brings a unique mix of aggression and sustainability to the battlefield. In this deep dive, we explore if this axe-wielding hamster still has the teeth to compete in the modern era of Empires & Puzzles or if he's better off running on a wheel.
Chomper Review: The Hamster That Hits Back
Chomper: The Axe-Wielding Hamster Who Heals by Hurting

Meet Chomper, the 5-star Fire Paladin from the Mighty Pet family who proves that big things come in small, furry packages. Released back in late 2022, this hamster isn't here to run on a wheel; he's here to smash faces and patch up his friends with the life essence of his enemies. He is a hybrid hero, functioning primarily as a damage dealer who hits three targets while providing significant healing to his team based on the damage he inflicts. While his cuteness is undeniable, we are here to see if his bite is still as bad as his bark in the current meta.

Chomper's design is a delightful contradiction. He is a fluffy, adorable guinea pig (or hamster, the debate rages on) decked out in barbarian-style armor, wielding a massive battle axe that looks heavy enough to crush a tank. The war paint on his face says "I am death," while his twitchy nose says "do you have any carrots?". It is a fantastic visual design that leans heavily into the whimsy of the Mighty Pets event without sacrificing the "warrior" aesthetic. The juxtaposition of a prey animal being an apex predator on the battlefield is comedy gold.

When we look at Chomper's stats in the context of the modern game (January 2026), the age cracks are starting to show. With a base power of 876 and stats hovering around the 850-860 mark, he falls significantly behind the latest releases which boast power levels well over 1300 and stats breaking the 1100 threshold raw. He is sturdy enough thanks to his Paladin class, but he lacks the raw stat stick power of the newer "god-tier" heroes. He is no longer a stat monster, but his distribution is balanced enough to keep him functional if you invest heavily in limit breaking.
"Slice and Dice" is a skill that rewards aggression.
Hitting the target and nearby enemies (Hit-3) for 330% is a solid, standard multiplier. It is not earth-shattering compared to modern snipers or full AoE nukers, but it is reliable damage.
This is the crown jewel of his kit. Heals each ally with 40% of the

. This is a dynamic heal. If you buff Chomper's attack or drop the enemy's defense, his healing output skyrockets. Conversely, if he hits a hero with high defense or defensive buffs, the healing whimpers.

The bleed damage is a nice cherry on top. It is "true damage" in a sense that it ticks away regardless of defense once applied, but in the fast-paced modern meta, damage-over-time is often cleansed before it can run its full course.

The mechanic of healing based on damage dealt makes him unique compared to fixed % healers. It allows him to turn a devastating offensive turn into a full team recovery, essentially acting as a "vampiric" support for the whole squad.
Let's break down the math behind the hamster.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Mighty Pet family is all about the interplay between Minions and Fiends.

The bonus improves Minions summoned for the hero (more stats) and weakens Fiends cast on the hero (less attack/health absorption). While useful, Chomper himself does not summon minions, so he relies on teammates to activate the minion part of this bonus. The fiend protection is nice but situational.
Punishes enemy fiend summoners. It's a "stop hitting yourself" mechanic.

This is the money maker. Gaining 5% Health and Mana when an ally gets a minion makes him synergize beautifully with minion heavy teams (e.g., Waterpipe, Freya, Miriam & Midnight). It effectively speeds him up and keeps him sustained.

simply increases the damage (and consequently the healing) of his first special skill activation. It helps ensure that first hit really counts, potentially topping off your team when they need it most. It's simple but effective for his specific mechanic.

Upon release, Chomper was a breath of fresh air. A heavy hitting red Paladin that could replace a healer in offensive stacks was highly coveted. He offered role compression (damage + healing) that few other heroes could match.

The game has gotten faster and stats have exploded. 330% damage to three targets is now "average" at best, and his base stats leave him vulnerable to one-shots from modern snipers. His healing, while potentially high, is inconsistent compared to modern "Overheal" healers or flat % healers who don't care about enemy defense stats.

. He is no longer a top-tier defense hero. However, in wars and raids on offense, he is still a fantastic tool, especially against older teams or in "Buff Booster" tournaments where his damage can be ramped up. He has transitioned from a "must-have" to a "solid depth" hero.
Chomper's healing is directly tied to his damage output. Therefore, there is only one logical path.
Maximizes the damage of "Slice and Dice," which in turn maximizes the healing received by the team. Also increases the bleed damage slightly.
Slightly less bulky, though the Paladin class helps mitigate this.
Keeps him alive longer to fire more often.
Reduces his healing output significantly, making his special skill feel underwhelming.
. A Chomper that hits like a wet noodle heals like a wet noodle. You need him to hit hard to justify his spot on the team.
Acts as both a damage dealer and a healer, freeing up a slot for another support or utility hero.
With the right setup (Defense Down, Attack Up), he can heal the team for 1000+ HP, rivaling dedicated healers.
Gains free health and mana if paired with minion summoners.
High natural defense and the chance to get a defense buff on taking damage makes him sturdier than he looks.
Healing is tied to damage. If he is blinded, hits a target with Dodge/Counterattack, or hits a high defense target, the healing is negligible.
In a Very Fast meta, Average speed can sometimes be too slow without mana support.
Low base stats compared to 2024/2025 releases mean he needs Limit Breaking to survive.
His bleed damage is easily cleansed.
Great for red stacks, especially against green heavy flanks/tanks.
His high damage potential here translates to massive healing.
His hit-3 and heal keeps the team topped up while clearing waves efficiently.
Note: His healing does NOT work here, making him just a damage dealer. He loses half his value.
The key to unlocking Chomper is
Drop the enemy defense first. Chomper hits much harder on defense-down targets, resulting in massive healing for the team, while softening them up for a slower finisher like Khufu.
Waterpipe summons minions frequently. Chomper's passive gives him mana every time a minion is summoned, effectively increasing his mana speed.
High defense reduces Chomper's damage and thus his healing.
Chomper will hurt himself badly and while he heals from the damage dealt, the reflect damage might kill him first.
If he misses, he deals 0 damage and heals for 0.
Do not bring him thinking he is a loophole. No healing means no healing.

If you pulled Chomper early, rejoice! He will carry you through the map seasons and be a cornerstone of your raid team. His ability to keep your team alive while killing enemies is invaluable for a developing roster. Prioritize leveling him and give him your Paladin emblems.

Chomper is likely a depth hero for you now. He is excellent for your 3rd or 4th War team or for Tower events where you need sustain. Do not prioritize Alpha Aethers for him unless you absolutely love his design or lack modern red options. He does not hold up well on defense in the top 500 alliances anymore.
The community has always had a soft spot for Chomper, mostly due to his visuals.
Players initially laughed at the hamster design but quickly realized he hit harder than he looked.

There was much discussion on the forums about whether his healing was better than fixed healers. The consensus: "High risk, high reward." As one user noted, "With EDD (Elemental Defense Down), you can hit something like 600 HP for 3 enemies... heal something like 720 HP for allies."

He is often compared to Sekhmet (Season 5). While Sekhmet is a sniper (hit-1), Chomper is a splasher (hit-3). Many prefer Chomper for the ability to impact more of the board, even if the single-target damage is lower.

Chomper remains one of the most charming heroes in Empires & Puzzles. He represents a time when event heroes were quirky and introduced fun new mechanics like "damage-to-heal" without completely breaking the game. While he may not be the terrifying monster he was in 2022, he is still a very capable combatant who can swing a match with a single, well-timed axe swing. He is a good boy, and yes, he deserves all the carrots.
A solid, reliable offensive hero who has aged gracefully into a support-attacker role. Keep him for offense, keep him for the cuteness, but keep him away from the top-tier defenses.

A comprehensive review and strategy guide for Chomper, the 5-star Fire Paladin from the Mighty Pet family. We analyze his stats, 'Slice and Dice' skill, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
